perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean any propr ietary information belonging to Discloser relating to : (i) Discloser’s proprietary technology , systems, service offering and products, including without limitation, plans and specifications, technical data, trade secrets, know -how, research, product plans, ideas or
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean any propr ietary data belonging to Discloser relating to : (i) Discloser’s proprietary technology , systems, service offering and products, including without limitation, plans and specifications, technical data, trade secrets, know -how, research, product plans, ideas or
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' is problematic. The contract defines 'Confidential Information' and uses that definition extensively. Switching the term in the definition creates uncertainty on whether 'data' is different from 'information' elsewhere in the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two (2) years from the dat e of receipt, all Confidential I nformation shall be maintained in confidence by Recipient , shall not be disclosed , published, communicate d, divulged or revealed in any manner, to any other person, firm, corporation or other third party entity, including employees of Recipient who do not have a need to know , or who should not know any such details pertaining to Confidential Information in the course of their employment , except as may otherwise provided in this Agreement. Recipient further affirmatively agrees not to use any Confidential I nformation received from Discloser except for the express purposes set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information'. The modified definition makes it unclear whether the obligations regarding 'Confidential Information' apply equally to 'data', potentially narrowing the scope of protection. The explanation clarifies that this creates uncertainty, fulfilling the criteria for a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction where the inconsistent use of 'information' and 'data' creates conflicting interpretations of the contractual obligations, specifically regarding what Recipient is obligated to protect.

perturbation.original_text: For the purpose of the Agreement, written Business or Technical I nformation (including that which is delivered electronically) that is considered to be C onfidential Information by Discloser, shall be so marked by Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purpose of the Agreement, written Business or Technical Materials (including that which is delivered electronically) that is considered to be C onfidential Information by Discloser, shall be so marked by Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Information' to 'Materials' within the section describing how Confidential Information is identified creates confusion. It's unclear if previously defined Confidential Information, which used the term 'Information', now falls under 'Materials'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two (2) years from the dat e of receipt, all Confidential I nformation shall be maintained in confidence by Recipient , shall not be disclosed , published, communicate d, divulged or revealed in any manner, to any other person, firm, corporation or other third party entity, including employees of Recipient who do not have a need to know , or who should not know any such details pertaining to Confidential Information in the course of their employment , except as may otherwise provided in this Agreement. Recipient further affirmatively agrees not to use any Confidential I nformation received from Discloser except for the express purposes set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Information' to define what needs to be marked as confidential. The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Materials'. The contradicted text refers to 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because it is now unclear whether previously identified 'Confidential Information' (based on the original definition using 'Information') now falls under the new definition using 'Materials'. This misalignment creates uncertainty and a potential contradiction, as the recipient's obligation to protect 'Confidential Information' is now ambiguous regarding what constitutes 'Confidential Information'.

perturbation.original_text: This information is to be t reated as Confidential Information by Recipient from the time of receipt of sai d notice. That being said, Recipient shall make every reasonable effort to mark all written copies of such information under its control as “Confidential” or “Proprietary” and to make every reasonable effort to inform Discloser of any disclosures by Recipient to third parties of such information that may have occurred between the time of receipt of the information and receipt by Recipient of notice that such information is confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: This data is to be t reated as Confidential Information by Recipient from the time of receipt of sai d notice. That being said, Recipient shall make every reasonable effort to mark all written copies of such information under its control as “Confidential” or “Proprietary” and to make every reasonable effort to inform Discloser of any disclosures by Recipient to third parties of such information that may have occurred between the time of receipt of the information and receipt by Recipient of notice that such information is confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' in the first sentence of section 3, but not in subsequent sentences of the same section, creates a contradiction. The section now refers to both 'data' and 'information' seemingly interchangeably, while only 'Confidential Information' is defined using the term 'information'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Disclose r may give notice in writing that written or verbal information that has been previously furnished, but not declared confidential, is in fact confidential. This data is to be t reated as Confidential Information by Recipient from the time of receipt of sai d notice. That being said, Recipient shall make every reasonable effort to mark all written copies of such information under its control as “Confidential” or “Proprietary” and to make every reasonable effort to inform Discloser of any disclosures by Recipient to third parties of such information that may have occurred between the time of receipt of the information and receipt by Recipient of notice that such information is confidential. Recipient has no responsibility to attempt to control the use o f such information by such a third party.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'information' with 'data' in the first sentence of section 3, while the subsequent sentences in the same section and the contradicted text retain the word 'information'. This creates a contradiction because 'Confidential Information' is defined using the term 'information', and the modified section now uses 'data' and 'information' seemingly interchangeably without defining them, as specified in the Misaligned Terminology definition of In-Text Contradiction. This implies that 'data' is now also considered 'Confidential Information' despite the original definition, leading to confusion and uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: All tangible information, including drawings, specifications and other information furnished hereunder s hall remain the property of Discloser. Upon request, or if either party e lects not to pursue any further business undertaking with the other , Recipient shall promptly return all tangible information, including any and all c opies or partial copies thereof and thereupon confirm destruction of all information held electronically.
perturbation.changed_text: All tangible documents, including drawings, specifications and other information furnished hereunder s hall remain the property of Discloser. Upon request, or if either party e lects not to pursue any further business undertaking with the other , Recipient shall promptly return all tangible information, including any and all c opies or partial copies thereof and thereupon confirm destruction of all information held electronically.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'documents' at the start of the section introduces contradiction. The section refers back to returning 'tangible information' later. If 'documents' and 'information' are distinct, there's ambiguity on what must be returned.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All tangible documents, including drawings, specifications and other information furnished hereunder s hall remain the property of Discloser. Upon request, or if either party e lects not to pursue any further business undertaking with the other , Recipient shall promptly return all tangible information, including any and all c opies or partial copies thereof and thereupon confirm destruction of all information held electronically.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'information' consistently, while the changed text replaces the initial instance with 'documents'. The contradicted text, which is identical to the original, relies on the original definition of 'information'. According to the explanation, this change introduces a contradiction because the contract refers back to 'tangible information' later in the section, and if 'documents' and 'information' are distinct, there's ambiguity on what must be returned. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to potentially conflicting obligations.
